items.title: Costa del Sol, Málaga & Granada, Spain - 2019 Trip - Day Four: Malaga Airport Museum – Part Two
items.summary: What I had planned to be a “cheap as chips” four day…
items.content_html:
What I had planned to be a “cheap as chips” four day break to my favourite quick & easy destination, Malaga, Spain, turned out to be more of a travel trip instead of lying on the beach everyday! when I was planning to go I thought just one day I’d like to travel out to somewhere out of Costa del Sol, I was looking into going to Granada and visiting the wonderful Alhambra but wow, I didn’t realise how popular this place is! just to book tickets into the most famous part of the palace, the ‘Plaza de Nazaríes’, you need to book three months in advance due to the Spanish limiting the number of tourists who can visit in a day – I had one week to find a ticket!!! did I make it? just by the skin of my teeth I found one opening on the Thursday I was going to be there so I snapped it up and sorted out my coach ticket 🙂

As well as spending an extremely long day visiting Granada (I was up at 5am to catch a train to Malaga, then a 2.5 hr coach ride to the city), I also checked out my favourite cosmopolitan city, Malaga, where I was staying Benalmadena, Fuengirola via a boat trip and a surprise last day I visited the amazing Malaga Airport Museum which was a bit of a find near the existing airport where I left my bag in storage before I headed back home to blighty!

As ever – enjoy the photos from this great trip! 🙂

items.title: Barcelona 2013: The Gaudi Experience
items.summary: Gaudi, the name you hear often when mentioning Barcelona, and quite rightly…
items.content_html:
Gaudi, the name you hear often when mentioning Barcelona, and quite rightly so. One visit to the fantasy buildings of Park Güell and you can enjoy the serenity of this man’s clever architectural design, I actually felt as if I was an extra in the Hansel and Gretel fairy tale! take a look at picture 24 towards the end of this set to see what I mean.

His most famous building the La Sagrada Famila, which is still being built today, left me blow away when I walked up the steps from the Sagrada Família metro for the first time, pictures don’t really do it justice, from the extraordinary Passion Facade figures on the outside to the beautiful colour’s coming off the stained windows once our inside ~ what a masterpiece!

La Sagrada Famila Photos

Park Guell Photos

Palau Guell Photos

Guell Pavilions Photos

Casa Mila (La Pedrera) Photos

Casa Batllo Photos

items.title: Barcelona 2013: Montserrat & Montjuic
items.summary: Taking a welcome trip out the hustle & bustle of central Barcelona…
items.content_html: Taking a welcome trip out the hustle & bustle of central Barcelona I visited Santa Maria de Montserrat Abbey which is about an hour by train from central. The mountain monastery  is another awe-inspiring place to check out and you getting to it requires a ride on a cable car (or funicular if you don’t like heights), after a visit to the church and taking in the views I headed up on the other funicular and into the mountain trails, climbing around those in just trainers was a memory I’ll never forget! but let’s just say there were a few ‘slip here and it’s adios amigo’ moments..
